turns.text: Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help you?
Yeah hi, I've got a drain that's completely backed up and I am NOT happy. This is the third time this month!
Oh no, three times in a month, that's really frustrating. Which drain is giving you trouble?
The kitchen sink. Water's just sitting there. I've got dishes piled up, dinner to cook, and I can't use my sink!
I totally get it, that's the worst timing. Hey, before we schedule anything, can I ask you something real quick?
What.
Do you have a plunger handy? Sometimes with kitchen sinks, a good plunge can clear it right up.
I mean... yeah, I have one. You think that'll actually work?
It's worth a shot! Fill the sink with a couple inches of water, then give it like fifteen to twenty good plunges. I'll hold on.
Fine. Hold on... [pause] ...okay I'm trying it... come on... come ON...
You got it, keep going!
Oh! Oh wait, it's— it's draining! Holy crap it's actually going down!
Ha! There you go! That's what I like to hear.
Wow, okay. I feel kinda dumb now. Sorry I was so snappy earlier.
Oh please, don't even worry about it. A backed-up sink will put anyone in a mood. If it backs up again, give us a call and we'll get someone out there.
I appreciate that, Lisa. Thank you so much.
Anytime! Have a good evening.
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, what can I do for you?
Hi, um, I'm not really sure what's going on but my sink is making a weird humming noise? And nothing's going down.
Hmm, a humming noise. Is that when you flip the switch for the garbage disposal?
The... yeah, the switch by the sink. It hums but nothing happens. Is it broken?
It might just need a reset! There's a little red button on the bottom of the disposal unit under the sink. Have you ever seen that?
Under the sink? Like... I have to open the cabinet?
Yep! Open the cabinet, look up at the bottom of the disposal. You should see a small red or black button.
Okay hold on let me look... I see a bunch of pipes... oh wait. Is it this little red thing?
That's the one! Go ahead and push it in.
Okay, I pressed it. Now what?
Now try the switch again, but make sure you run some water first.
Alright... water's running... flipping the switch... OH! It's working! It's grinding!
There we go! Sometimes they just trip and need that little reset.
I had no idea that button was even there. I was about to buy a whole new one!
Ha, glad we saved you that trip! If it keeps tripping though, that could mean something else, so just give us a ring.
I definitely will. Thank you, you're a lifesaver!
Happy to help! Have a great day.
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help?
Hello, yes, I am calling because my toilet, it does not stop the water. It is always running, you know?
Oh sure, a running toilet. Yeah, that sound drives you crazy, right?
Yes, yes, all night I hear it. Very loud. Is this... is big problem?
Usually it's something simple! Can I walk you through a quick fix real fast?
Okay, I try.
Great. So on top of the toilet, there's a handle you push to flush, right? Try jiggling that handle, like push it up and down a few times.
The... the lever? The thing for flush?
Yes, exactly, the flush lever! Just wiggle it around a little.
Okay, I do it now... I move it... up, down...
Does the water sound stop?
Wait... ah... yes! Ah yes, ok, the water stop! It is quiet now!
Perfect! That means the flapper inside is a little worn. It's a cheap part if you ever want us to swap it out.
The flapper? What is this?
It's a little rubber piece inside the tank. If the jiggle trick stops working, just call us and we can replace it for you. Very quick job.
Ah yes, ok, thank you. Thank you very much. You are very kind.
Aw, you're welcome! Call anytime. Bye bye!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Hi yeah quick question, I've got a leak under my bathroom sink and I need someone out here. What's your earliest?
Let me check for you real quick. Is the leak pretty bad or more of a slow drip?
It's a steady drip, I've got a bucket under there. It's not an emergency but I don't wanna leave it too long.
Smart. Okay so I've got Thursday morning open, would that—
Hold on— sorry, I'm getting another call. Can you— actually, you know what, let me call you back. I gotta take this.
No problem at all! We're here till five. Talk soon!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help you today?
Hello? Yes, hello dear. I'm not sure if I should be calling you or... I think I smell gas? In my kitchen?
Ma'am, if you're smelling gas, I need you to leave the house right now. Don't touch any light switches, just get outside.
Oh my, really? I thought maybe it was the stove, I was trying to—
Yes ma'am, please head outside right now and call 911 from out there. This is really important.
Well... oh, hold on, my son just pulled up in the driveway actually.
Oh good! Tell him right away about the gas smell and get outside together, okay?
He's coming in now. Michael! Michael, come here— I think— yes, he says he smells it too. He's going to handle it, dear. Thank you.
Okay, please make sure you both get outside. And if you need a plumber after the gas company checks it, call us anytime. Stay safe!
Thank you, sweetheart. Bye now.
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help?
Yeah, I had an appointment last week for my water heater and your guy showed up an hour and a half late. No call, nothing.
Oh gosh, I am so sorry about that. That's not okay at all. Can I pull up your account?
Marcus Webb. And I took the whole morning off work for this. I want a refund.
I completely understand, Marcus. Let me look at your account here... I see the appointment. I'm really sorry about the experience.
Sorry doesn't really cut it. I lost half a day of pay sitting around waiting.
You're absolutely right, and I'd be upset too. I can process a full refund for that visit. Would that work?
Yeah, I want the refund. And honestly, I had another appointment booked for this Friday? Cancel that too. I'll find someone else.
I understand. I'll cancel Friday's appointment and get that refund started for you today. Again, I'm really sorry we let you down, Marcus.
Alright. Thank you.
You'll see the refund in three to five business days. Take care.
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Hi there. I'm just calling to get a ballpark on sewer line work. My neighbor just had his done and said I should get mine checked.
Sure thing! So it really depends on what's needed. A camera inspection to see what's going on usually runs about two fifty to four hundred.
Okay. And if it actually needs to be repaired?
A spot repair could be anywhere from a thousand to three thousand. A full line replacement, you're looking more like five to fifteen thousand depending on length and access.
Wow. Okay. And does the inspection cost go toward the repair if I end up needing it?
Yep! If you book the repair with us, we credit the inspection fee. A lot of folks like that.
That's good to know. Let me talk to my wife about it and I'll probably call back to schedule that inspection.
Sounds great, no rush at all. We're here whenever you're ready!
Thanks, Lisa. Bye.
Bye bye!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help?
Hello, yes. I want to change my... the water, the thing in the kitchen, the...
The faucet? The part where the water comes out?
Yes! Faucet, yes. I want new one. But I don't know which kind.
Oh sure! So there's a few types. Do you want one handle or two handles?
Hmm, what is... what is the difference?
One handle means you move it left and right for hot and cold. Two handles, you have a separate one for each.
Ah, I think one. More easy, yes?
Totally, single handle is super easy. And do you want the tall kind that pulls out, like a sprayer?
Pull out? What is this pull out?
It's where the faucet head comes off and you can spray around the sink. Like a little hose.
Oh! Yes, I see this in my friend house. Yes, I want that. How much is this?
With the faucet and installation, usually between two fifty and four fifty depending on the brand you pick.
Okay, I think about it. Maybe I come back, call back. Thank you for explain to me.
Of course! And we can help you pick one out when you're ready. Just give us a call!
Thank you, bye bye.
Bye!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Oh hi Lisa! So glad I got a real person. I hate those automated systems, you know? Anyway, I'm calling about my drain.
Ha, I'm definitely real! Which drain is acting up?

So it's the bathtub drain upstairs. And I gotta tell you, this thing has been a problem since we moved in back in 2019. The previous owners, I swear they must have had dogs or something because the first time we snaked it, you would not BELIEVE what came out.
Oh no, I can only imagine! So it's clogging up again?

Yeah, it's been draining slow for like a month and now it's basically standing water. My husband tried the Drano, which I told him not to, and then he tried the little snake from Home Depot, but it's one of those cheap plastic ones, you know the kind?
Oh yeah, those little zip-strip ones. They're okay for hair near the surface but they won't reach the real stuff.

Exactly! That's exactly what I said to him. So then my sister-in-law said her plumber used a camera and found tree roots in HERS, which, I mean our yard has that big maple so now I'm worried it's roots.
It's possible for sure, especially with a big tree. We could do a camera inspection and see exactly what's going on in there.
Yeah, see, that's what I was thinking. But then my husband's like 'just call a plumber to snake it' and I'm like, but what if it's roots? You know what I mean?
Totally. The camera inspection takes the guessing out of it. We'd know for sure.
How much is that?
The inspection is two fifty, and if we do the repair the same visit, we credit that toward the work.
Hmm. Two fifty just to look at it... Let me think about it. I might just try the snake one more time first.
Totally understand, no pressure at all! We're here whenever you need us.
Actually... you know what, no. I've been going back and forth on this for a month already. Let's just do it. What do you have available?
Ha, let's get it done! I've got Wednesday afternoon or Friday morning. Either of those work?
Wednesday's perfect. I'm off Wednesdays.
Awesome. Can I get your name and address?
Denise Kowalski. K-O-W-A-L-S-K-I. We're at 842 Birchwood Lane.
Got it. And a good phone number?
555-0187. That's my cell, you can text too if you need to.
Perfect. So Denise, you're all set for Wednesday afternoon for a bathtub drain camera inspection at 842 Birchwood Lane. Our tech will call when they're on the way.
Great! And tell them to watch out for the maple tree roots, ha!
Ha, I'll make a note of it! See you Wednesday, Denise.
Thanks Lisa! Bye!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Hi I have a burst pipe in my basement and there's water everywhere. I need someone NOW.
Oh my gosh, okay. First thing, do you know where your water main shutoff is? We need to stop that water.
Yeah I already shut it off. Water's stopped but there's like two inches on the floor already.
Good thinking shutting that off. Let me get someone to you ASAP. What's your name and address?
Ray Donovan, 1560 Oak Park Drive.
And best number to reach you?
Got it. I'm dispatching someone right now, Ray. They should be there within the hour. Emergency rate is one seventy-five for the first hour, does that work?
Yeah that's fine, just get them here. I gotta start getting this water up.
Absolutely. Ray Donovan, 1560 Oak Park Drive, burst pipe, tech on the way. If you have a wet vac, start with that. Hang tight!
Thank you. Bye.
Bye, help is on the way!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help you today?
Hello dear. I have an appointment on Thursday, but I need to cancel it if that's alright.
Of course! Can I get your name so I can look that up?
It's Dorothy Haines. H-A-I-N-E-S. I had a toilet that was giving me trouble.
I see it right here, Dorothy. Thursday at ten for a toilet repair. Is everything okay?
Oh yes, everything's fine! My son came over this weekend and he fixed it himself. He's very handy, you know.
Aw, that's wonderful! Sounds like you've got a good one.
He really is. I hated to bother him but he insisted. He replaced some part inside the tank, the little rubber thing?
The flapper! Yep, that's usually the culprit. I'm glad he got it taken care of for you.
Yes, that's it. So I don't need the appointment anymore. I'm sorry for the trouble.
No trouble at all, Dorothy. I'll get that canceled for you right now. And if anything else comes up, you know where to find us.
You're so sweet. Thank you, dear. Have a lovely day.
You too, Dorothy! Take care now.
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Yeah hi, my garbage disposal is completely dead and I need someone out here today.
Oh no, sorry to hear that! Let me take a look at the schedule... so the earliest I have is Thursday morning.
Thursday?! That's three days from now! You're kidding me.
I know, I'm sorry, we've been really slammed this week. I wish I had something sooner.
So what am I supposed to do for three days with no disposal? I cook every single night!
I totally hear you, that's a pain. What I can do is put you on our cancellation list and if anything opens up before Thursday, you'd be first call. Sometimes we get same-day openings.
A cancellation list. So I just... sit and wait and hope someone cancels.
I know it's not ideal. But people do cancel, especially day-of. And either way you'd have Thursday locked in as a backup.
[sigh] Fine. Put me on the list AND book Thursday.
Done and done. Can I get your name and info?
Victor Reyes. 2200 Magnolia Street. 555-0341.
Got it. So Victor, you're on the cancellation list starting now, and you've got Thursday morning at nine for the garbage disposal. We'll call as soon as anything opens up.
Alright. Thursday at nine. And you better call me if something opens up.
You'll be my first call, Victor. I promise. Hang in there!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help?
Hey Lisa! So I've got a water heater situation. But first, let me give you some background because I think it's relevant.
Sure, go ahead!

So we bought this house in 2016, right? And the inspector said the water heater was quote-unquote 'near end of life' but that was eight years ago and it was still going! So I figured, why replace it if it's working?
Ha, if it ain't broke, right?

Exactly! But here's the thing. Last winter it started making this rumbling sound, like a low gurgle? My buddy Frank — he used to do HVAC, not plumbing, but close enough — he said it was probably sediment buildup.
Yeah, Frank's probably right on that one. Sediment's super common in older tanks.

So I drained it, followed a YouTube video, the whole nine yards. Worked great for a few months. But NOW, and this is the part that worries me, the water is coming out kinda rusty looking? Like brownish? Only from the hot side.
Ooh, rusty water just from the hot side, that usually means the anode rod is shot and the tank might be starting to corrode inside.
See, that's what I was afraid of. And I was reading online that once you see rust, it's basically— oh shoot, someone's at the door. Hang on...
Sure, take your time!
Oh it's the Amazon guy and I gotta sign for this. Hey Lisa, I'm gonna have to call you back, is that okay?
Absolutely, no worries! I'm here till five, just call back whenever.
You're great, thanks! Bye!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa!
Hello. I have problem, I think, with the sewer. My toilet and the shower, both drain very slow now.
Oh boy, both of them at the same time? That does sound like it could be a sewer line issue.
Sewer line... this is expensive, yes? My neighbor, he pay ten thousand dollar.
It can be, but it really depends on what's going on. We'd start with a camera inspection to see what the problem actually is. That's only two fifty.
Two fifty for just to look? And then maybe ten thousand more? I... I don't know. Maybe I wait.
I understand, that's a lot to think about. But here's the thing — sometimes it's just a clog or a small issue that's way cheaper to fix. The inspection tells us for sure.
But what if it IS the big problem?
Then at least you know, and you can plan for it. And we offer payment plans too. But it might not even be that bad!
Payment plan... okay. This is... you can do monthly?
Yep, monthly payments, no problem. But let's not worry about that yet. Let's just do the inspection first and see what we're dealing with.
Okay. Yes. You are right. Let's do the inspection. Better to know.
Smart decision! What's your name?
Tomasz Nowak. T-O-M-A-S-Z. Nowak is N-O-W-A-K.
Got it, Tomasz. And your address and phone number?
411 Elm Court. Phone is 555-0462.
I have Tuesday morning or Wednesday afternoon open. Which is better for you?
Tuesday, in the morning, yes.
Perfect. So Tomasz, you're all set for Tuesday morning for a sewer line camera inspection at 411 Elm Court. Our tech will call before they head over.
Okay. Thank you, Lisa. You make me feel better about this.
Aw, that's sweet, thank you! We'll take good care of you, Tomasz. See you Tuesday!
Smith Plumbing, this is Lisa, how can I help?
Hi, um, so I have water on my ceiling? Like a wet spot? I don't know if this is a plumbing thing or a roof thing.
Oh no. Is the wet spot near a bathroom or anywhere with pipes above it?
Actually yeah, the upstairs bathroom is right above it. Does that mean something?
It could definitely be a plumbing leak from that bathroom. Is the spot getting bigger or staying the same?
I mean... I noticed it a few days ago and it might be a little bigger? I'm not sure. Is this like an emergency or...?
It's not a drop-everything emergency, but I wouldn't wait too long either. Water damage can get worse fast.
Okay, that's kinda what I was worried about. Can you send someone to look at it?
I'd love to, but honestly we're pretty booked up right now. The soonest I've got is next Monday.
Monday? But you just said it could get worse fast...
I know, I'm sorry about the timing. Here's what I'd suggest — try not to use that upstairs bathroom until we get there, especially the shower and tub. That should slow things down.
Just... don't use the bathroom?
The toilet's fine, it's got its own seal. But avoid the shower and tub for now. And if that spot on the ceiling starts dripping or gets a lot bigger, call us back and we'll treat it as an emergency.
Okay. Okay, that makes sense I guess. Let's do Monday then.
Alright, great. Can I get your name?
Janelle Brooks.
And your address and phone number, Janelle?
720 Sycamore Drive. 555-0518.
Got it. So Janelle, you're booked for Monday morning for a leak inspection at 720 Sycamore Drive. Remember, if the ceiling gets worse before then, call us right away.
I will. Thanks for explaining everything, I feel a lot less panicked now.
That's what I'm here for! See you Monday, Janelle. Take care.
